steps.title: Identify the sine ratio and find AC
steps.content:
From the figure, triangle ABC has its right angle at C, and the hypotenuse AB is labeled 26. In a right triangle, the sine of an angle equals the opposite leg over the hypotenuse, so:
$$\sin(B) = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{5}{13}$$
Substituting AB = 26:
$$\frac{AC}{26} = \frac{5}{13}$$
$$AC = 26 \cdot \frac{5}{13} = 10$$

steps.title: Use the Pythagorean theorem to find BC
steps.content:
With AC = 10 and AB = 26, apply the Pythagorean theorem:
$$AC^2 + BC^2 = AB^2$$
$$10^2 + BC^2 = 26^2$$
$$100 + BC^2 = 676$$
$$BC^2 = 576$$
$$BC = \sqrt{576} = 24$$
So BC = 24.
